input.background: Another way to look at the data presented in Exercise 14.6 is to restrict the analysis to a range that can be described with a straight line. This is called a range restriction.
input.question: Calculate $r^2$ for this range restricted model. Which model has a better fit, this model for the ln-ln transformed model from the previous exercise?

output.method: 基于准则的比较（AIC， BIC等）
output.answer: $r^2=0.856$. The fit of this model is not as good as the $\ln -\ln$ model calculated in Exercise 14.6, in which $r^2=0.947$.

range_restricted_r_squared.value: Not explicitly provided; assumed to be calculated based on the range-restricted model.
range_restricted_r_squared.description: The coefficient of determination (r^2) for the range-restricted model.

ln_ln_transformed_r_squared.value: Not explicitly provided; assumed to be calculated based on the ln-ln transformed model from the previous exercise.
ln_ln_transformed_r_squared.description: The coefficient of determination (r^2) for the ln-ln transformed model.

model_type.description: The type of model being compared.

meta_info.caption: Domestic water and dental cavities, range restriction.
